Package org.areslib.command

Class CommandScheduler

java.lang.Object
org.areslib.command.CommandScheduler
public final class CommandScheduler extends Object
The scheduler responsible for running Commands. A Command-based robot should call run() in its periodic block in order to run commands synchronously.

Uses standard Java logic and handles generic subsystems and commands.

  • Method Details

    • addButton

      public void addButton(Runnable button)
      Registers a button binding loop to be executed periodically by the scheduler.
      Parameters:
      button - a Runnable representing the button's polling loop.

    • getInstance

      public static CommandScheduler getInstance()
      Returns the Singleton instance of the scheduler.
      Returns:
      the instance

    • registerSubsystem

      public void registerSubsystem(Subsystem... subsystems)
      Registers subsystems with the scheduler. This must be called for the subsystem's periodic block to run when the scheduler is run, and for the subsystem's default command to be scheduled.
      Parameters:
      subsystems - the subsystem to register

    • unregisterSubsystem

      public void unregisterSubsystem(Subsystem... subsystems)
      Un-registers subsystems with the scheduler. The subsystem's periodic will no longer run, and its default command will no longer be scheduled.
      Parameters:
      subsystems - the subsystem to un-register

    • getSubsystems

      public Set<Subsystem> getSubsystems()
      Returns an unmodifiable set of all registered subsystems. Useful for external backend enumerators like physical simulators.
      Returns:
      The current value.

    • setDefaultCommand

      public void setDefaultCommand(Subsystem subsystem, Command defaultCommand)
      Sets the default command for a subsystem. The default command will run whenever there is no other command currently scheduled that requires the subsystem.
      Parameters:
      subsystem - the subsystem whose default command should be set
      defaultCommand - the default command to associate with the subsystem

    • schedule

      public void schedule(Command command)
      Schedules a command for execution.
      Parameters:
      command - the command to schedule

    • cancel

      public void cancel(Command command)
      Cancels a command. The scheduler will call its end() method and un-schedule it.
      Parameters:
      command - the command to cancel

    • isScheduled

      public boolean isScheduled(Command command)
      Checks whether a specific command is currently scheduled and running.

      This is the safe way to test if a separately-scheduled command has completed, as opposed to calling command.isFinished() directly, which can race with the scheduler's own removal logic.

      Parameters:
      command - The command to query.
      Returns:
      True if the command is currently in the scheduled commands set.

    • run

      public void run()
      Runs a single iteration of the scheduler. The execution occurs in the following order:

      1. Subsystem periodic methods are called.

      2. Default commands are scheduled for subsystems that have no acting command.

      3. Button binding loops are polled.

      4. Scheduled commands' execute() methods are called.

      5. Commands that have finished their run are removed, and their end() methods are called.

    • cancelAll

      public void cancelAll()
      Cancels all currently-scheduled commands.

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Fully resets the scheduler, clearing ALL registered subsystems, default commands, button bindings, and scheduled commands. This MUST be called between OpMode transitions (e.g., Auto → TeleOp) to prevent duplicate subsystem registrations and stale bindings.
